factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Off (Iran Deal)
factors.summary: Trump announced framework agreement with Iran, canceling military strikes. Markets rallied hard Thursday; Nikkei +4%, South Korea Kospi +8%, gold +3%, oil fell to $85. Dow components (BA, CAT, transports) benefit from reduced supply-chain disruption risk.

factors.name: Hot US PPI Data
factors.summary: US Producer Price Index came in hotter than expected in May, per FXStreet and investinglive reports. This raises input-cost pressure for Dow industrials (CAT, HON, GE) and supports higher-for-longer rates narrative, capping multiple expansion.

factors.name: US Dollar Strength
factors.summary: DXY trades near 99.80, gaining ground on hot PPI and Middle East uncertainty. Strong USD headwind for Dow's ~50% international revenue exposure (MSFT, GE, CAT, BA); multinational translation drag.

factors.name: Oil Price Stabilization
factors.summary: WTI steadies near $85 after Trump's Iran deal comments. Lower energy costs benefit transportation/logistics (UPS, FDX not in Dow but signals cost relief), but Chevron (CVX) in Dow sees margin compression; net modest tailwind given Dow's light energy weighting.

factors.name: Copper & Industrial Metals Rebound
factors.summary: Copper rallied from 3-week low after Trump signaled Iran deal. Signals improved demand outlook for CAT, CMI, industrial construction; easing recession fears among Dow cyclicals.

factors.name: Rate Expectations / Fed Policy
factors.summary: No new FOMC data in window, but hot PPI raises probability of held rates longer. ECB hiked rates first time since 2023, supporting dollar. 10-year yield stable; no major shift to confirm dovish repricing yet.

factors.name: Deal Reality Check / Iran Skepticism
factors.summary: Multiple reports flag Iran Supreme Leader hasn't blessed deal, IRGC rhetoric remains combative, Hormuz tanker reportedly blocked. Trump's 39th 'deal' announcement history. De-escalation narrative faces credibility risk if deal falls apart over weekend.

factors.name: Corporate Profit Cycle & Earnings Resilience
factors.summary: Bloomberg report: corporate profits hit record highs vs. India economy; companies upbeat on demand and capex despite stock struggles. Dow's heavyweight earnings (UNH, GS, JPM, HD, V) historically stable; supports defensive holding.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO / Tech Spectacle Momentum
factors.summary: SpaceX $75B IPO (largest ever) and Nasdaq 100 additions (Rocket Lab, Astera, CoreWeave, AI names) generating retail/foreign investor flows. Positive sentiment spillover to tech-adjacent Dow names (MSFT, AAPL proximity), though Dow itself is industrials-heavy.

factors.name: Adobe CFO Exit / Execution Concerns
factors.summary: Adobe loses second top exec; stock at 7-year low. Signals management/strategy uncertainty in high-growth names; may spill into broader tech/software confidence, indirectly affecting MSFT sentiment.

screening.narrative: The Dow rallied +1.25% in 24h on Iran deal hopes and geopolitical de-escalation, with Nikkei and Kospi surging harder on reduced Middle East supply-chain risk. However, the rally is already ~90min into Phase 2 execution, and hot US PPI data is creating a countercurrent by reinforcing higher-for-longer rates and raising input-cost pressures on Dow industrials. Dollar strength (DXY 99.80) is a headwind for multinational revenue translation. The deal remains fragile—Iran's Supreme Leader has not officially blessed it, and IRGC rhetoric suggests leverage-building rather than surrender, raising overnight-to-weekend flip risk.
catalysts_ahead: Iran deal signing claim/confirmation (weekend, high flip risk)
PBOC monetary policy and yuan management (ongoing, China demand proxy)
RBA decision June 16 (holds expected; forward guidance on inflation/rates)
BOK rate decision next month (Shin hinting hikes; supports KRW, rate-sensitive Dow plays)
Dow component earnings: major banks (JPM, GS), healthcare (UNH), retail (HD) in coming weeks
US CPI/PCE inflation data (next releases post-PPI; rate-sensitive equities pivot point)
ISM Services PMI (mid-June; Dow cyclical confidence)
screening.risk_warning: Iran deal collapse or escalation (drone interception reported, Hormuz blockade threat) would reverse de-escalation rally and spike oil/VIX, hammering Dow's industrial leaders. Dollar unwinding on deal peace-and-calm could flip the headwind. Hot PPI repeat could force higher-rates repricing, capping multiples on dividend/value stocks (banks, utilities). Dow component earnings misses or guidance cuts would derail earnings resilience narrative.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already in PHASE 2 (rally underway ~18h into catalyst); avoid chasing. Wait for pullback to 50,600–50,700 support or a close above 51,000 on Iran deal confirmation before adding. If deal falters overnight, 50,400–50,500 becomes tactical entry for reversal.

factors.name: Iran deal optimism & oil crash
factors.summary: Trump announced framework agreement with Iran; Brent crude fell below $90/bbl on ceasefire hopes and Hormuz reopening prospects (Societe Generale, Deutsche Bank, ING reports). Lower energy input costs and reduced inflation shock support equities and reduce Fed hike urgency.

factors.name: Hot US PPI inflation data
factors.summary: US PPI came in hotter than expected (mentioned in NZD/USD and Canadian Dollar reports); this contradicts the dovish tilt from oil collapse and could extend Fed tightening cycle. Core inflation pressures persist.

factors.name: ECB hawkish hold + July optionality
factors.summary: ECB hiked 25bps and signalled readiness for second hike in July if energy shock persists (Nagel, Makhlouf). While markets price two more hikes, ECB avoided clear guidance (Commerzbank), creating uncertainty. Euro rally (EUR/USD to 1.1600) signals some risk-off easing but Fed path remains uncertain.

factors.name: S&P 500 strong rally, largest in 2 months
factors.summary: Deutsche Bank noted S&P 500 posted largest gain in two months on lower oil + reduced Fed hike expectations. Risk sentiment has improved markedly from geopolitical fears; broader equity momentum favours US30 participation.

factors.name: Dow 4h/24h price action already up +0.52%/+1.25%
factors.summary: Rally is already underway; move has not extended dramatically but is material. Suggests Phase 2 consolidation or mild pullback likely before further acceleration. Momentum intact but near-term overhead building.

factors.name: UK GDP -0.1% m/m (weak), services slump
factors.summary: UK economy marginally contracted in April as services sector cooled. Signals developed-market growth fragility; less relevant to US30 directly but feeds global risk narrative if spread continues.

factors.name: Dollar strength (DXY ~99.80) on hawkish inflation
factors.summary: USD Index holding elevated levels despite Iran deal relief, driven by hot US PPI and reduced Fed dovish bets. Strong dollar historically headwind for Dow multinationals (~50% international revenue); translation drag likely.

factors.name: SpaceX blockbuster IPO (+35% shadow trading indicated)
factors.summary: Historic $75B IPO launches today; broad retail/institutional enthusiasm boosts sentiment and liquidity. However, mega-cap IPO tail-risk (volatility, capital rotation from dividend Dow names) is real. Musk mania may elevate tech over cyclicals.

factors.name: Core inflation edging up in EU (Germany +2.5%, France +2.4%, Spain +3.0%)
factors.summary: European core inflation accelerating despite oil relief, suggesting secondary effects from energy shock are broadening. Supports ECB's hawkish tilt and complicates growth outlook. Negative for global cyclical demand and Dow industrials exposure.

factors.name: Fed forward guidance and rate cut expectations
factors.summary: No fresh Fed communication in this window; prior messaging saw rate hikes fading on oil collapse but hot PPI has relit some hawkishness. Kevin Warsh (incoming Chair) not yet signalled policy direction. Ambiguity caps confidence.

factors.name: Dow heavyweight earnings & guidance absent
factors.summary: No recent earnings surprises or forward guidance from major Dow components (UNH, MSFT, GS, CAT, JPM, BA) in this window. Earnings calendar opacity limits conviction on cyclical outlook.

factors.name: China growth data (PBOC CNY setting, general Asia momentum)
factors.summary: PBOC set USD/CNY reference at 6.8109 vs 6.7640 estimate (CNY slightly weaker, neutral). No China PMI or growth data in window; Asia equities rallied on Iran deal but no fundamental China refresh on CAT/DE demand drivers.

key_levels.support: 50,600 (psychological, recent swing low near June 11 close)

screening.narrative: US30 is riding a relief rally on Iran deal optimism and oil-price collapse (Brent <$90), which eases inflation/stagflation fears and loosens Fed hike expectations. S&P 500 posted its biggest gain in 2 months on this backdrop. However, the move is already +1.25% in 24h (Phase 2), and hot US PPI data plus ECB hawkish-hold messaging re-introduce rate and growth uncertainty. SpaceX's blockbuster IPO today adds sentiment lift but also tail risks of capital rotation away from dividend-weighted Dow. Dollar strength persists despite oil relief, headwind for multinational translation. Near-term consolidation likely before next directional move.
catalysts_ahead: SpaceX IPO opening today (sentiment driver, potential retail inflows vs rotation from Dow)
RBA decision June 15–16 (likely hold; guidance on inflation/hikes critical for AUD/USD and risk sentiment)
US economic calendar: CPI/PCE, durable goods, factory orders in coming days (could reignite Fed tightening debate if hot)
Iran deal finalization or collapse (binary event; no ceasefire = oil re-spike, risk-off, Dow weakness)
ECB July meeting (July 3–4) — ECB signalled readiness for second hike; market expectations vs reality could move risk assets
Fed speakers (Kevin Warsh, Powell, other governors) — incoming Chair's first signals on policy path critical
screening.risk_warning: If Iran deal collapses or stalls, oil spikes back above $100, triggering stagflation fears and sharp equity selloff (Dow down 2–3%). If US PPI/CPI surprise to the hot side next week, Fed hawkishness re-emerges and rate-cut expectations collapse, crashing dividend/cyclical names and Dow hard. If SpaceX IPO flops or triggers sector rotation away from industrials/financials, momentum breaks. If China growth disappoints, CAT/BA/DE earnings guidance weakens, cyclical confidence erodes.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (+1.25% in 24h); wait for consolidation/pullback toward 50,600–50,700 before fresh long entries. If break above 51,100 on volume, trail stops tight and take profits into resistance. Avoid chasing into SpaceX euphoria; focus on Dow-specific drivers (earnings, Fed path, dollar strength).
